BugInstance.ShortMessage: Public static method may expose internal representation by returning a mutable object or array
BugInstance.LongMessage: Public static spark.ExceptionMapper.getInstance() may expose internal representation by returning ExceptionMapper.defaultInstance

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Class using singleton design pattern has non-private constructor.
BugInstance.LongMessage: Class (spark.ExceptionMapper) using singleton design pattern has non-private constructor.

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Be wary of letting constructors throw exceptions.
BugInstance.LongMessage: Exception thrown in class spark.QueryParamsMap at new spark.QueryParamsMap(HttpServletRequest) will leave the constructor. The object under construction remains partially initialized and may be vulnerable to Finalizer attacks.

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Method with Boolean return type returns explicit null
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.QueryParamsMap.booleanValue() has Boolean return type and returns explicit null

BugInstance.ShortMessage: May expose internal representation by returning reference to mutable object
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.Request.bodyAsBytes() may expose internal representation by returning Request.bodyAsBytes

BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.Request.headers() may expose internal representation by returning Request.headers

BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.Request.session() may expose internal representation by returning Request.session

BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.Request.session(boolean) may expose internal representation by returning Request.session

BugInstance.ShortMessage: May expose internal representation by returning reference to mutable object
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.Response.raw() may expose internal representation by returning Response.response

BugInstance.LongMessage: Inconsistent synchronization of spark.Service.initialized; locked 75% of time

BugInstance.LongMessage: Inconsistent synchronization of spark.Service.webSocketIdleTimeoutMillis; locked 50% of time

BugInstance.ShortMessage: May expose internal representation by incorporating reference to mutable object
BugInstance.LongMessage: new spark.Service$StaticFiles(Service) may expose internal representation by storing an externally mutable object into Service$StaticFiles.this$0

BugInstance.ShortMessage: May expose internal representation by returning reference to mutable object
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.Session.raw() may expose internal representation by returning Session.session

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Method invokes System.exit(...)
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.embeddedserver.jetty.EmbeddedJettyServer.extinguish() invokes System.exit(...), which shuts down the entire virtual machine

BugInstance.ShortMessage: May expose internal representation by incorporating reference to mutable object
BugInstance.LongMessage: new spark.embeddedserver.jetty.EmbeddedJettyServer(JettyServerFactory, Handler) may expose internal representation by storing an externally mutable object into EmbeddedJettyServer.handler

BugInstance.ShortMessage: May expose internal representation by incorporating reference to mutable object
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.embeddedserver.jetty.EmbeddedJettyServer.configureWebSockets(Map, Optional) may expose internal representation by storing an externally mutable object into EmbeddedJettyServer.webSocketHandlers

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Redundant nullcheck of value known to be non-null
BugInstance.LongMessage: Redundant nullcheck of webSocketServletContextHandler, which is known to be non-null in spark.embeddedserver.jetty.EmbeddedJettyServer.ignite(String, int, SslStores, int, int, int)

Method.Message: In method spark.embeddedserver.jetty.EmbeddedJettyServer.ignite(String, int, SslStores, int, int, int)

Method.Message: Return value of spark.embeddedserver.jetty.websocket.WebSocketServletContextHandlerFactory.create(Map, Optional) of type org.eclipse.jetty.servlet.ServletContextHandler

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Inefficient use of keySet iterator instead of entrySet iterator
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.embeddedserver.jetty.websocket.WebSocketServletContextHandlerFactory.create(Map, Optional) makes inefficient use of keySet iterator instead of entrySet iterator

BugInstance.LongMessage: Switch statement found in spark.resource.UriPath.canonical(String) where default case is missing

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Load of known null value
BugInstance.LongMessage: Load of known null value in spark.route.Routes.findMultiple(HttpMethod, String, String)

BugInstance.LongMessage: Public static spark.route.ServletRoutes.get() may expose internal representation by returning ServletRoutes.routes

BugInstance.ShortMessage: May expose internal representation by incorporating reference to mutable object
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.serialization.Serializer.setNext(Serializer) may expose internal representation by storing an externally mutable object into Serializer.next

BugInstance.LongMessage: Inconsistent synchronization of spark.staticfiles.StaticFilesConfiguration.externalStaticResourcesSet; locked 50% of time

BugInstance.LongMessage: Inconsistent synchronization of spark.staticfiles.StaticFilesConfiguration.staticResourceHandlers; locked 46% of time

BugInstance.LongMessage: Inconsistent synchronization of spark.staticfiles.StaticFilesConfiguration.staticResourcesSet; locked 50% of time

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Field isn't final but should be
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.staticfiles.StaticFilesConfiguration.servletInstance isn't final but should be

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Reliance on default encoding
BugInstance.LongMessage: Found reliance on default encoding in spark.utils.IOUtils.copy(InputStream, Writer): new java.io.InputStreamReader(InputStream)

BugInstance.ShortMessage: compareTo()/compare() incorrectly handles float or double value
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.utils.MimeParse$FitnessAndQuality.compareTo(MimeParse$FitnessAndQuality) incorrectly handles float value

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Class defines compareTo(...) and uses Object.equals()
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.utils.MimeParse$FitnessAndQuality defines compareTo(MimeParse$FitnessAndQuality) and uses Object.equals()

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Inefficient use of keySet iterator instead of entrySet iterator
BugInstance.LongMessage: spark.utils.MimeParse$ParseResults.toString() makes inefficient use of keySet iterator instead of entrySet iterator

BugInstance.ShortMessage: Reliance on default encoding
BugInstance.LongMessage: Found reliance on default encoding in spark.utils.StringUtils.toString(byte[], String): new String(byte[])

BugInstance.ShortMessage: May expose internal representation by incorporating reference to mutable object
BugInstance.LongMessage: new spark.utils.urldecoding.Utf8StringBuilder(int) may expose internal representation by storing an externally mutable object into Utf8StringBuilder._buffer

LocalVariable.Message: Local variable stored in JVM register ?

BugPattern.ShortDescription: May expose internal representation by incorporating reference to mutable object
BugPattern.Details: This code stores a reference to an externally mutable object into the internal representation of the object.  If instances are accessed by untrusted code, and unchecked changes to the mutable object would compromise security or other important properties, you will need to do something different. Storing a copy of the object is better approach in many situations.

BugPattern.ShortDescription: Inefficient use of keySet iterator instead of entrySet iterator
BugPattern.Details: This method accesses the value of a Map entry, using a key that was retrieved from a keySet iterator. It is more efficient to use an iterator on the entrySet of the map, to avoid the Map.get(key) lookup.

The variable referenced at this point is known to be null due to an earlier check against null. Although this is valid, it might be a mistake (perhaps you intended to refer to a different variable, or perhaps the earlier check to see if the variable is null should have been a check to see if it was non-null).

BugPattern.ShortDescription: Field isn't final but should be
BugPattern.Details: This public static or protected static field is not final, and could be changed by malicious code or by accident from another package. The field could be made final to avoid this vulnerability.

BugPattern.ShortDescription: Public static method may expose internal representation by returning a mutable object or array
BugPattern.Details: A public static method returns a reference to a mutable object or an array that is part of the static state of the class. Any code that calls this method can freely modify the underlying array. One fix is to return a copy of the array.

BugPattern.ShortDescription: Method with Boolean return type returns explicit null
BugPattern.Details: A method that returns either Boolean.TRUE, Boolean.FALSE or null is an accident waiting to happen. This method can be invoked as though it returned a value of type boolean, and the compiler will insert automatic unboxing of the Boolean value. If a null value is returned, this will result in a NullPointerException.

BugPattern.ShortDescription: Redundant nullcheck of value known to be non-null
BugPattern.Details: This method contains a redundant check of a known non-null value against the constant null.

BugPattern.ShortDescription: Be wary of letting constructors throw exceptions.
BugPattern.Details:
Classes that throw exceptions in their constructors are vulnerable to Finalizer attacks

A finalizer attack can be prevented, by declaring the class final, using an empty finalizer declared as final, or by a clever use of a private constructor.

See SEI CERT Rule OBJ-11 for more information.

BugPattern.ShortDescription: Class defines compareTo(...) and uses Object.equals()
BugPattern.Details:
This class defines a compareTo(...) method but inherits its equals() method from java.lang.Object. Generally, the value of compareTo should return zero if and only if equals returns true. If this is violated, weird and unpredictable failures will occur in classes such as PriorityQueue. In Java 5 the PriorityQueue.remove method uses the compareTo method, while in Java 6 it uses the equals method.

From the JavaDoc for the compareTo method in the Comparable interface:

It is strongly recommended, but not strictly required that (x.compareTo(y)==0) == (x.equals(y)). Generally speaking, any class that implements the Comparable interface and violates this condition should clearly indicate this fact. The recommended language is "Note: this class has a natural ordering that is inconsistent with equals."

BugPattern.ShortDescription: May expose internal representation by returning reference to mutable object
BugPattern.Details: Returning a reference to a mutable object value stored in one of the object's fields exposes the internal representation of the object.  If instances are accessed by untrusted code, and unchecked changes to the mutable object would compromise security or other important properties, you will need to do something different. Returning a new copy of the object is better approach in many situations.

BugPattern.ShortDescription: Class using singleton design pattern has non-private constructor.
BugPattern.Details:
This class is using singleton design pattern and has non-private constructor (please note that a default constructor might exist which is not private). Given that, it is possible to create a copy of the object, thus violating the singleton pattern.
The easier solution would be making the constructor private.

SEI CERT MSC07-J rule

BugPattern.ShortDescription: compareTo()/compare() incorrectly handles float or double value
BugPattern.Details: This method compares double or float values using pattern like this: val1 > val2 ? 1 : val1 < val2 ? -1 : 0. This pattern works incorrectly for -0.0 and NaN values which may result in incorrect sorting result or broken collection (if compared values are used as keys). Consider using Double.compare or Float.compare static methods which handle all the special cases correctly.

Found a call to a method which will perform a byte to String (or String to byte) conversion, and will assume that the default platform encoding is suitable. This will cause the application behavior to vary between platforms. Use an alternative API and specify a charset name or Charset object explicitly.

The fields of this class appear to be accessed inconsistently with respect to synchronization.  This bug report indicates that the bug pattern detector judged that

  • The class contains a mix of locked and unlocked accesses,
  • The class is not annotated as javax.annotation.concurrent.NotThreadSafe,
  • At least one locked access was performed by one of the class's own methods, and
  • The number of unsynchronized field accesses (reads and writes) was no more than one third of all accesses, with writes being weighed twice as high as reads

A typical bug matching this bug pattern is forgetting to synchronize one of the methods in a class that is intended to be thread-safe.

You can select the nodes labeled "Unsynchronized access" to show the code locations where the detector believed that a field was accessed without synchronization.

Note that there are various sources of inaccuracy in this detector; for example, the detector cannot statically detect all situations in which a lock is held.  Also, even when the detector is accurate in distinguishing locked vs. unlocked accesses, the code in question may still be correct.

Invoking System.exit shuts down the entire Java virtual machine. This should only been done when it is appropriate. Such calls make it hard or impossible for your code to be invoked by other code. Consider throwing a RuntimeException instead.

BugPattern.ShortDescription: Switch statement found where default case is missing
BugPattern.Details:
This method contains a switch statement where default case is missing. Usually you need to provide a default case.

Because the analysis only looks at the generated bytecode, this warning can be incorrect triggered if the default case is at the end of the switch statement and the switch statement doesn't contain break statements for other cases.

BugCode.Description: Method returning array may expose internal representation
